title: The Saplings Listening at the Root-Margin

features.name: saplings in rows
features.desc-on-touch: Young trees lean toward the old roots underground, listening for the chorus. They have not yet learned to sing; they are still learning the words in root-language.

features.name: root teaching point
features.desc-on-touch: Where old and new roots meet, the archive records an exchange. The chorus does not translate; it only teaches by letting young wood absorb the song through the earth.

features.name: chorus resonance
features.desc-on-touch: A pool of soil where frequencies from below become visible as ripples. You can hear the old eucalypti singing through the saplings that learn their song.

features.name: growth ring archive
features.desc-on-touch: Each ring the saplings lay down is a note in the chorus. The archive files them all; someday, the young trees will understand what they were always singing.

inscription: the saplings have not yet joined the chorus, but they are already learning the harmonies through their roots; they sing by growing in the exact shape the old song requires

file.name: Sapling_RootMargin_SongAbsorption_GrowthRecords.txt
file.note: How young trees learn to move toward the frequency of old roots beneath them, laying down rings that are notes in a chorus none of them can hear above ground.

exits.label: back toward the old chorus singing deeper
deeper into the root dark where the oldest song begins
toward the congregation where all roots become one voice
